(likely FastAPI/Starlette from JSON 404 shape), static inline HTML/JS, POST JSON endpoint /jobs, unknown database/backing store. SURFACE: GET / returns HTML with filter form; POST /jobs Content-Type application/json body {"job_type": }; common paths probed (/robots.txt,/api,/admin,/debug,/healthz,/login*,/search*,/profile*,/users,/user,/sitemap.xml,/.git/HEAD,/backup) all 404 JSON. BUG-HYP: IDOR/access-control bug in /jobs filter: UI exposes job_type options including "private" and endpoint may return private jobs/flag without authentication; possibly JSON field tampering can enumerate hidden records. WHY: Landing page markup includes selectable option value="private" and table renders id/name/type/description returned from /jobs; challenge category is idor; no auth/session observed; app exposes server-side data via a single filter parameter. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: /jobs trusts client-supplied job_type/type/id and discloses unauthorized private records (evidence: HTML option "private", POST /jobs JSON route, no auth cookies). - ATTACK CHAIN: fingerprint /jobs baseline → enumerate accepted JSON keys/values and IDs → retrieve private/flag-containing job → flag. - FIRST PROBE: reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9101 - ABANDON IF: /jobs rejects private/extra fields uniformly and endpoint inventory shows a different live oracle.
